description: Baseline: charset scanning throughput at 29.75ns per operation (aws keys mixed benchmark)

asi.hypothesis: Baseline measurement for SIMD charset scanner
asi.test_case: AWS keys mixed (14KB data, repeated AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE patterns)

description: Opt 1: 4x loop unrolling in scalar_token_end - 33% faster (19.87ns vs 29.75ns baseline)

asi.hypothesis: 4x loop unrolling improves ILP in scalar path

description: Opt 2: 8x loop unrolling in scalar_token_end - 46% faster (15.96ns vs 29.75ns baseline)

asi.hypothesis: 8x loop unrolling increases parallelism and reduces branch misses

description: Opt 3: 16x loop unrolling - 47% faster (15.74ns), but diminishing returns vs 8x

asi.hypothesis: 16x unrolling saturates ILP, minimal improvement over 8x

description: Opt 4 (REVERTED): Bitmap charset - slower (21.3ns), bitwise overhead dominates

asi.hypothesis: Bitmap representation reduces cache footprint but adds bitwise operations
asi.observation: Bitwise ops overhead greater than cache benefit

description: Opt 5 (REVERTED): Full LTO slower than thin (16.34ns vs 15.94ns)

asi.hypothesis: Full LTO should improve throughput but introduces overhead
asi.observation: Thin LTO is better than full for this micro-benchmark
